pages.text: A cobblestone generator is the old reliable: bring flowing water and flowing lava together with one empty block between them, and stone forms in the gap — ready to mine, again and again. Vanilla taught you this.$(br2)Novus keeps it exactly so, then adds one rule: the block $(bold)underneath$() decides what you get.
pages.title: Cobblestone Generators

pages.name: Basic Generator
pages.text: Water and lava meet over the gap; cobblestone forms where they touch. The block one space below that gap is what the generator reads.

$(bold)The block below.$() Whenever lava and water create a block, Novus checks the block one space $(bold)beneath$() the spot where it forms. That single block is the whole rule.$(br2)Set the right block there and the same generator hands you something better than cobblestone — no extra cost, no machinery.
pages.title: The Block Below

pages.text: $(bold)Two substrates work at any height$() — so these can sit on the surface:$(br2)$(li)$(bold)Diorite$() (plain or polished) below → the generator yields $(bold)Andesite$()$(li)$(bold)Sandstone$() — plain, cut, chiselled, and the red variants — below → it yields $(bold)Tuff$()$(br2)Both are a deliberate head start for $(italic)Create$(): andesite and tuff are the bedrock of its early recipes.
pages.title: Andesite & Tuff

pages.name: Andesite Generator
pages.text: Set $(bold)diorite$() one block below the gap and the same setup yields $(bold)andesite$() in place of cobblestone — at any height, surface included.

pages.name: Tuff Generator
pages.text: Swap the block below for $(bold)sandstone$() and the generator yields $(bold)tuff$(). Any sandstone variant counts — plain, cut, chiselled, or red — and height does not matter.

$(bold)The third rule needs depth.$() $(bold)Below Y=1$() — true deepslate country — a generator standing on ordinary stone or cobblestone converts over:$(br2)$(li)stone becomes $(bold)Deepslate$()$(li)cobblestone becomes $(bold)Cobbled Deepslate$()$(br2)At Y=1 or above, plain stone and cobble below simply give you ordinary cobblestone.
pages.title: Deepslate Depth

pages.text: $(bold)In short — the block below the gap:$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Diorite$() → Andesite (any height)$(li)$(bold)Sandstone$() → Tuff (any height)$(li)$(bold)Stone or cobble, below Y=1$() → Deepslate / Cobbled Deepslate$(li)$(bold)Anything else$() → ordinary cobblestone$(br2)All of this is overworld-only. In the Nether and the End a generator behaves exactly as in vanilla.
pages.title: In Short
